name: ExLlamaV2
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); requires NVIDIA GPU; no API costs

performance.notes: Fastest GPTQ inference engine; 20-40% faster token generation than vLLM for GPTQ models on consumer GPUs

agent_readiness.notes: Highly optimized CUDA inference engine for GPTQ and EXL2 quantized LLMs. Fastest inference for quantized models on consumer NVIDIA GPUs. EXL2 format allows mixed-precision quantization for better quality/speed tradeoff. Used as backend in text-generation-webui and TabbyAPI. Continuous batching. Flash Attention 2. Primarily used for local LLM serving on enthusiast hardware.

gotchas: NVIDIA GPU required — no CPU or AMD support
Documentation sparse — primarily README and example scripts
EXL2 quantization format not widely supported outside exllamav2 ecosystem
Less community support than vLLM or llama.cpp
best_when: Maximum inference speed for GPTQ models on consumer NVIDIA GPUs
Local LLM enthusiasts on 24GB VRAM cards (RTX 4090)
Backend for text-generation-webui or TabbyAPI setups
EXL2 quantization for optimal quality/speed tradeoff
summary: ExLlamaV2 is the fastest GPTQ/EXL2 inference engine for NVIDIA GPUs, achieving 20-40% faster token generation than alternatives on consumer hardware. Supports the EXL2 mixed-precision quantization format. Used as backend in text-generation-webui and TabbyAPI for local LLM hosting. Best for enthusiast setups on 24GB VRAM consumer cards. Sparse documentation; not recommended for production deployments.
